title: Victims Come Forward In Hopes To Ban Non-Disclosure Agreements | 10 News+
description: Three women - from three different workplaces - who claim they were sexually harassed, join forces to try ban non-disclosure agreements silencing victims.  Corrective Services NSW spokesperson: “We have a zero-tolerance policy towards sexual assault and harassment in our workplaces. We recognise the strength and courage of victim-survivors who have experienced harm at the hands of these individuals. CSNSW are not involved in the process of NDAs for victims-survivors. We encourage any staff member who is aware of misconduct in the workplace to report it to Professional Standards and Investigations or to NSW Police so we can continue to identify and act upon instances of wrongdoing.”   Defence spokesperson: “Defence continues to work to prevent sexual violence, and acting on incidents sensitively, diligently and in a trauma-informed way. Where reports are received Defence takes action through a trauma-informed approach. Support is always available to our people. In circumstances where sexual violence has occurred or is alleged, Defence places considerable weight on the wishes of the claimant, including where the claimant wishes to maintain their privacy.”   Attorney-General Michelle Rowland spokesperson: “All Australian workplaces should be safe and free from sexual harassment. The Government’s policy is generally not to seek confidentiality in the settlement of workplace sexual assault or sexual harassment claims, and to only agree to confidentiality where this is requested by the claimant. Confidentiality will not be used to prevent a claimant from being able to tell their story. The upcoming statutory review of the Anti-Discrimination and Human Rights Legislation Amendment (Respect at Work) Act 2022 (Cth) may provide an opportunity to consider further the use of non-disclosure agreements in workplace sexual harassment cases.”   Join the conversation and follow us on: X: https://x.com/10NewsAU Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/10NewsAU/ Instagram: http://www.instagram.com/10NewsAU/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@10NewsAU LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/10newsau  10 News airs at 5pm, seven nights a week in your local market on Network 10. title: Australian Woman Creates 2,682 Personalities To Protect Her From Abuse | 10 News+
description: You've probably heard of people who have multiple personalities; the term for it now is Dissociative Identity Disorder, and the woman you're about to meet has more than 2,500 different personalities. Jeni Haynes created them to protect her from the horrific abuse she suffered during her childhood, and in a world first, an Australian court heard and accepted evidence from six of those identities, which led to her father being sentenced to 45 years in prison.
